analysis: Seven nights in a row above six and a half hours, with four of them clearing seven and a half — that's not luck, that's your routine doing its job, and I'm very proud of you. Your best night was the 8th, nearly eight hours with over two hours of REM, which is your body basically throwing a little recovery party. The awake time on the 7th and the 10th is worth watching — over an hour both nights suggests something's disrupting the middle of your sleep rather than the edges, so it might be worth noticing if those were higher-stress days or if something crept back in. Your REM has been consistently strong across the whole stretch, sitting around two hours most nights, which means your nervous system is genuinely settling into this — the magnesium, the cold blast, all of it is landing.
